executiveSummary.text: Gilead's HIV franchise remains highly cash-generative, long-acting prevention (lenacapavir) is a genuine breakthrough, and the oncology expansion via Trodelvy is gaining traction, but the liver disease franchise faces generic headwinds. HOLD is the weighted recommendation with a conviction score of 61.

businessModel.text: Gilead Sciences Inc (GILD) operates in the Healthcare sector and is listed on NASDAQ. The business generates revenue through its core operations in healthcare.

recentCatalysts.text: Gilead Sciences Inc has been navigating the current market environment with its core healthcare franchise. Key developments over the past 3-6 months include ongoing execution of strategic priorities, macroeconomic sensitivity assessments in relevant end markets, and competitive positioning updates. Investors should monitor quarterly earnings reports and any material company announcements for the most current view on near-term catalysts.

thesisEvaluation.text:
Bull Case (76): Lenacapavir achieves wide uptake in both prevention and treatment settings globally. Trodelvy succeeds in earlier-line breast cancer indications. HBV functional cure programmes produce Phase III data. EPS grows to $8.50+. Price: $170+.
            Base Case (62): HIV franchise holds steady with Biktarvy growth offsetting Truvaya/Descovy generic pressure. Trodelvy grows but faces competitive ADC dynamics. Liver disease revenues stabilise. EPS: $7.00-7.80. Price: $140-165.
            Bear Case (43): Lenacapavir uptake slower due to pricing/access barriers. Trodelvy fails in key Phase III readouts. Generic competition to liver disease drugs accelerates faster than modelled. EPS falls to $5.50-6.00. Price: below $115.
            Weighted Score: 62 — Bull 76 × 30% + Base 62 × 50% + Bear 43 × 20%

bull.scenario: Bull Case (76): Lenacapavir achieves wide uptake in both prevention and treatment settings globally. Trodelvy succeeds in earlier-line breast cancer indications. HBV functional cure programmes produce Phase III data. EPS grows to $8.50+. Price: $170+.

base.scenario: Base Case (62): HIV franchise holds steady with Biktarvy growth offsetting Truvaya/Descovy generic pressure. Trodelvy grows but faces competitive ADC dynamics. Liver disease revenues stabilise. EPS: $7.00-7.80. Price: $140-165.

bear.scenario: Bear Case (43): Lenacapavir uptake slower due to pricing/access barriers. Trodelvy fails in key Phase III readouts. Generic competition to liver disease drugs accelerates faster than modelled. EPS falls to $5.50-6.00. Price: below $115.

risks.risk: HIV franchise competitive entry: GSK/ViiV's long-acting cabotegravir directly competes with lenacapavir in prevention. Pricing and access negotiations could limit peak penetration.

Trodelvy competitive dynamics: The ADC space in oncology is rapidly becoming crowded (AstraZeneca/Daiichi, Gilead's ownpipeline). Confirmatory trials and survival data may not support current trajectories.
Biktarvy patent cliff: While Biktarvy is protected into the early 2030s, any future generic entry would represent a significant revenue event for the core HIV business.
Liver disease revenue volatility: Revenue from HCV treatments is declining as the treated patient pool normalises. NASH/ASH programmes remain speculative.
Acquisition integration and capital allocation: Gilead's $21bn Immunomedics acquisition has performed better than feared but the high acquisition price constrains management flexibility.

recommendation.text:
HOLD. BUY below $115 (implies 17x P/E on base-case EPS — attractive for a cash-generative franchise with lenacapavir optionality).
            Reduce above $155 (implies 23x P/E pricing in optimistic lenacapavir scenarios).

entryExit.text:
Gilead Sciences Inc (GILD) — Entry / Exit Framework

HOLD zone: $127.87–$141.77 — current levels are fairly valued. No compelling reason to add or trim here.
Add / Initiate below $127.87 — material pullback that improves risk/reward.
Trim / Take Profits above $141.77 — approaching the 52-week high at $157.29. Risk/reward no longer favourable for new capital.
Stop-loss consideration: A sustained close below $91.5 (52-week low) would warrant reassessment.
